summary: Regression testing of BLE disconnect-on-turn fix (protocol MOVE handler + try/catch in smartcube.js). Bluetooth path can't be exercised in-browser; used window.__trainer/__cube debug hooks. All 4 requested regression/stability tests passed with zero console errors and zero page errors.

test_results.test1_move_detection: PASS — feedFacelets(SOLVED) sets reference (state==SOLVED). After feedFacelets(applyMove(SOLVED,'R')) state CHANGED and equals expected next state. Timer switched waiting -> running, counted up to 0.7s.
test_results.test2_solve_advances_pair: PASS — solveCurrent() advanced pair KD -> IE, SOLVED counter=1, STREAK=1, timer immediately running for the new pair (0.4s).
test_results.test3_stability_10_moves: PASS — 10 random-face moves fed sequentially (R,U,U,D,R,R,D,D,R,D). getState matched applied final state exactly. No error overlay, no uncaught page errors, no console error messages.
test_results.test4_keyboard_manual_moves: PASS — pressing r/u/f changed cube state and kept the timer running (1.9s). No console errors.

critical_code_review_comments: Real BLE disconnect-on-turn bug is only reproducible with a physical GAN cube; automated environment can only confirm app-boundary regression, which is clean.
src/lib/smartcube.js try/catch around events$ subscribe is a good defensive fix; consider logging the caught error to console.warn so future handler bugs are visible without breaking BLE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a React SPA at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (no backend). Debug hooks available: window.__trainer.{getState,getTarget,solveCurrent,feedFacelets,markSolved} and window.__cube.{SOLVED,applyMove,applyAlg,scramble}. First feedFacelets call sets the reference. Timer testid: recognition-timer with data-timer-state; letter pair testid: letter-pair-display.
